home.intro: Kite Aventure offers lessons for beginners, intermediate, and advanced riders, as well as kite travel / retreat packages to dream destinations where a wetsuit is not necessary! Need lessons, a refresher, or on-water supervision? Contact us!

seo.title: Our Team of Certified Instructors | Kite Aventure
seo.description: Meet our certified kitesurfing instructors in Quebec City. A passionate team ready to help you discover kitesurfing.

members.name: Simon L'Allier
members.bio: Certified instructor. Passionate about kitesurfing for over 20 years. He is the instructor you need to progress safely. Always ready to share his passion for kitesurfing and introduce riders to new spots.

members.name: Jean-Luc Robitaille
members.bio: Certified instructor. Big air enthusiast. Expert in warm and exotic destinations. Never wears a long wetsuit.

members.name: Benoit Fournier
members.bio: Kite veteran. North Kiteboarding representative. The best kite repair technician in eastern Canada.

seo.title: Kitesurfing Lessons in Quebec City — 2026 Pricing | Kite Aventure

school.title: Kitesurfing School
school.subtitle: Kitesurfing lessons in the Quebec City region and L'Isle-aux-Coudres

pricing.title: 2026 Pricing
pricing.intro: To take kitesurfing lessons with Kite Aventure, packages of 3h, 6h and 9h are available (private or shared). Your registration payment puts you on Kite Aventure's selective student list, and lets you book a lesson time slot online when the wind looks favourable.
pricing.doubleNote: *A shared lesson includes 1 kite for 2 students.
pricing.capacityNote: We will only take about a dozen students this summer — hurry up and reserve your spot!!

conditions.title: Before registering, make sure you meet the following requirements:
items: Be 12 years of age or older
Know how to swim and be in adequate physical shape

Own your wetsuit (4:3 mm thickness recommended, 3:2 mm may work for those who don't get cold easily) and neoprene water shoes (no thick sole, or very thin and flexible sole — you need to feel the board under your feet)
Be able to travel to the practice locations (Quebec City region and L'Isle-aux-Coudres)

Know that you risk entering an obsessive cycle of pleasure and passion (read: checking wind forecasts 5 times a day, only talking about kite with your friends, missing family dinners because it's windy, being surrounded by fellow addicts who have the same problem as you, etc.)

included.note: We take care of the rest (helmet, PFD (personal flotation device), bar + lines, harness, kite, etc.)

register.title: How to Register?
register.step3: Send an Interac transfer to: info{'@'}kiteaventure.ca
register.step4: For more information, message us privately with your phone number so we can chat!

seo.title: Frequently Asked Questions | Kite Aventure
seo.description: Answers to the most common questions about kitesurfing lessons and trips with Kite Aventure in Quebec City.

items.question: What happens if weather conditions are not suitable for my lesson? (no wind, too much wind, bad weather)
items.answer:
The safety of our students is our top priority — we never go out on the water in unsafe conditions.

If your lesson must be cancelled due to unfavourable weather conditions (insufficient wind, wind that is too strong or gusty, thunderstorms, etc.), we will rebook your lesson as soon as possible. You keep your spot at the very top of our priority list — you will be among the first to be contacted when conditions are ideal.

If we are unable to reschedule and provide your lesson, we will refund the full amount paid.

items.question: Kitesurfing Lesson Locations
items.answer:
Kite lessons take place at 3 different locations depending on wind direction, wind strength, and tide.

Southwest wind:

L'Isle-aux-Coudres (Halte des Piliers)
Google Maps link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/VeQaLERMpbs16FpV7
GPS coordinates: 47.36919065132224, -70.39422262256325

Note: a ferry ride is required to reach L'Isle-aux-Coudres. The wait for the ferry typically ranges from 15 to 45 minutes, depending on traffic. The ferry is free and takes approximately 30 minutes.

Here is the ferry location:
Google Maps link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/WnKniFMeY5MvoTuh8
GPS coordinates: 47.44950045662119, -70.36565254662186

Northeast wind:

Option 1: Baie de Beauport
Google Maps link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/YAVXF8Hik33aXm3Y8
GPS coordinates: 46.83597615923758, -71.20287543057084

Option 2: Berthier-sur-Mer (Plage de Berthier-sur-Mer)
Google Maps link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/U3x7roX61Kx41ano6
GPS coordinates: 46.93440319278342, -70.71650790220106
